summary: Mixed astronomical-cadastral register — 3 astro labels (kaki=Pleiades x2, maki=Mars x1, maqa=eclipse x4) + 3 cadastral labels (kaqa=wall x2, chiqa=truth x2, taqa implicit). 188 cords, 38 clusters, 1-PAIRE depth L4, 100% hit rate. First word chiqa (truth) = thesis is VERIFICATION. Interrogative pitaka (who-ACC-PASS, cord 107, GG) = formal inquiry. Most STRING on L2-L4 = words annotate numbers. Possibly an observation station survey (where + what to observe). Museo de la Nacion, Lima. 16 STRING.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  188 cords. 16 STRING (9%, 100% hits), 159 INT, 13 EMPTY (7%).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, depth L4. 38 clusters.
  Colors: AB(77), W(52), MB(15), RL(15), LA(10) — 5 colors.
  Provenance: Unknown. Museum: Museo de la Nacion, Lima.

HYBRID VOCABULARY
  Astronomical: kaki (Pleiades x2), maki (Mars obs x1), maqa (eclipse x4)
  Cadastral: kaqa (wall-AY x2), chiqa (truth x2)
  Interrogative: pitaka (who-ACC-PASS x1, GG color) = formal inquiry
  3 astro + 3 cadastral = mixed document.

FIRST WORD = chiqa (truth, C1, AB) — verification as thesis.
INTERROGATIVE = pitaka (C107, GG) — 'who was assigned/responsible?'
CLOSING = TOP-pa sv=50 (C188, W) — topic marker with value.

DUAL-LEVEL TEXT
  L1 (7 STRING): chiqa, pitaka, kaki, chi-DIR, maqa(sv=10), maqa, TOP-pa(sv=50) — PRIMARY labels.
  L2-L4 (9 STRING): kaki, kaqa, chi-GEN, maki, kaqa, maqa x2, chiqa, chi-DIR — ANNOTATIONS.
  Numbers primary, words explain = reference register format.

NARRATIVE — THE OBSERVATORY AND THE WALL
  Truth. That is the first word — *chiqa*, cord 1. The document opens as a certification. What follows is a register of numbers with scattered celestial and geographic labels. The Pleiades appear twice — once buried in the tree at L2, once prominently at L1. Mars appears once, also buried. The eclipse marker *maqa* appears four times, the most frequent label.
  At cord 107, on a gray cord of justice: *pitaka* — who was responsible? Who was assigned to this station? The formal interrogative, in passive-accusative.
  The walls (*kaqa*, Aymara) and the verification marks (*chiqa*) frame a document that is neither pure astronomy nor pure cadastre but something between — a register of what was observed and where, who tracked the Pleiades and who measured the walls.
  The museum says 'provenance unknown.' The vocabulary says: somewhere an observer watched the Pleiades rise above an Aymara wall and recorded both on the same khipu.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Mixed astro-cadastral analysis
Ceque system: huacas assigned to astronomical duties (Zuidema 1964)
